summary.difficultyAssessment: Distribution is D1:60(43%) D2:54(39%) D3:26(19%) — significantly skewed towards D1 vs the target ~30:40:30. D1 is 13 percentage points over target, D3 is 11 points under target. Many D1 questions rated as D1 that involve large multi-digit multiplication (e.g. Q82: 38x26x17, Q91: 44x30x19, Q99: 56x38x24, Q106: 64x46x30, Q115: 76x54x38) are arguably D2 since a 9-year-old needs to do substantial mental/written arithmetic. Conversely, several cube volume questions rated D1 with large cube numbers (Q50: 13^3=2197, Q59: 14^3=2744, Q70: 15^3=3375, Q78: 16^3=4096, Q92: 17^3=4913, Q102: 19^3=6859) should be D2 — knowing or computing these cube numbers is beyond easy recall for a Year 5 child.
summary.coverageAssessment: Good conceptual coverage across 8 sub-concepts: basic cuboid volume, cube volume, volume-to-capacity conversion, missing dimensions, comparing volumes, fraction of volume, scaling volumes, and real-world word problems. However, Q119-Q140 introduce genuinely new question types (L-shapes, T-shapes, step-shapes, packing cubes, displacement, flow rate problems) that have NO dedicated lessons or sub-concepts, representing a teaching gap. Composite/compound shapes (L-shaped, T-shaped, step-shaped solids) are a significant GL topic with no lesson coverage.
summary.teachingEfficacyAssessment: Lessons are well-structured with the 4-screen format (hook-teach-interact-consolidate) and good use of CuboidDiagram and WorkedExample visual components. However, 3 orphan sub-concepts (cube-root, volume-to-capacity, volume-word-problems) have lessons but their questions map to OTHER sub-concept IDs, meaning the mapping is inconsistent. The master-volume-cuboids sub-concept has 46 questions mapped to it but NO lesson — this is the single biggest teaching efficacy gap. Multiple sub-concepts have mismatched IDs between lessons and mappings.
topPriorities: Fix D1/D3 distribution imbalance — promote ~10 large-arithmetic D1 cuboid/cube questions to D2, and add ~14 more D3 questions to reach 30%
Create lesson content for master-volume-cuboids sub-concept (46 questions with no lesson)
Create lesson content for composite/compound shapes (L-shape, T-shape, step-shape, packing cubes) — Q126-Q133, Q139
Fix orphan sub-concept ID mismatches so volume-to-capacity and cube-root lessons connect to their questions

issues.title: D1/D3 difficulty distribution significantly off target
issues.description: Current distribution is D1:60(43%) D2:54(39%) D3:26(19%). Target is approximately 30:40:30. D1 is over-represented by 13 percentage points and D3 is under-represented by 11 points. This means the question bank is too easy overall — children will encounter disproportionately fewer challenging questions during practice.
issues.suggestedFix: Promote approximately 10 D1 questions to D2 (especially large-number cuboid calculations like Q82, Q86, Q91, Q94, Q99, Q101, Q106, Q109, Q115, Q118 where the arithmetic itself is challenging). Add approximately 14 new D3 questions or re-rate existing D2 questions that involve multi-step reasoning. Good D3 candidates from existing bank: Q129 (L-shaped composite, currently D2), Q130 (T-shaped composite, currently D2).

issues.title: master-volume-cuboids has 46 questions but NO lesson
issues.description: The largest sub-concept by question count (46 questions, 33% of all volume questions) maps to 'master-volume-cuboids' which has no lesson content. Every mapping for these questions shows '[No lesson found]'. This is a fundamental teaching gap — children encounter these questions in focused learning mode but receive no preparatory lesson.
issues.suggestedFix: Either create a dedicated lesson for basic cuboid volume calculation (l x w x h), or remap these questions to an existing sub-concept with lesson content. The simplest fix is creating a 'volume-cuboids' sub-concept with step-by-step and spot-the-mistake lessons covering the basic formula.

issues.title: volume-to-capacity has 31 questions but orphan lesson sub-concept
issues.description: 31 questions map to sub-concept ID 'volume-to-capacity', but the lesson sub-concept is stored under the ID 'volume-to-capacity (how much a container can hold)'. The ID mismatch means questions and lessons don't connect — '[No lesson found]' appears for all 31 questions despite comprehensive lessons existing for this exact topic.
issues.suggestedFix: Align the sub-concept IDs. Either rename the lesson sub-concept from 'volume-to-capacity (how much a container can hold)' to 'volume-to-capacity', or update the question mappings. The parenthetical description should be in the display name, not the ID.

issues.title: Cube of 13 rated D1 — too hard for easy
issues.description: Q50 asks for 13^3 = 2197, rated D1 (easy). Computing 13 x 13 x 13 without a calculator requires substantial arithmetic (13x13=169, 169x13=2197). This is not 'easy' for a 9-year-old. Similarly affects Q59 (14^3=2744), Q70 (15^3=3375), Q78 (16^3=4096), Q92 (17^3=4913), Q102 (19^3=6859).
issues.suggestedFix: Re-rate Q50, Q59, Q70, Q78, Q92, Q102 as D2. These cube numbers beyond 10^3 require significant computation and are not common knowledge for Year 5 children.

issues.title: Decimal multiplication rated D1 — too hard for easy
issues.description: Q97 asks for 7 x 5.5 x 3.5 = 134.75 cubic metres, rated D1. This involves multiplying two decimal numbers which is D2 difficulty at minimum. Similarly Q113 (5 x 4.5 x 3.5 = 78.75) is rated D1 but involves decimal multiplication.
issues.suggestedFix: Re-rate Q97 and Q113 as D2. Decimal multiplication with two decimal operands is not straightforward for a 9-year-old.

issues.title: Large multi-digit cuboid calculations rated D1
issues.description: Multiple questions from Q82 onwards involve large multi-digit multiplication rated as D1: Q82 (38x26x17=16796), Q86 (48x32x20=30720), Q91 (44x30x19=25080), Q94 (52x35x22=40040), Q99 (56x38x24=51072), Q101 (60x42x28=70560), Q106 (64x46x30=88320), Q109 (72x48x35=120960), Q115 (76x54x38=155952), Q118 (84x60x42=211680). While the concept is simple (LxWxH), the arithmetic complexity makes these unsuitable as D1.
issues.suggestedFix: Re-rate questions with triple-digit or large double-digit multiplications to D2. D1 cuboid questions should use simpler numbers where the arithmetic can be done mentally (e.g. 10x5x4, 6x3x2).

issues.title: Simple comparison question rated D3
issues.description: Q8 asks to compare an 8x8x8 cube (512cm3) with a 16x4x8 cuboid (512cm3). While the concept of 'equal volumes from different shapes' is a higher-order idea, the arithmetic here is straightforward and the answer is the most obvious choice. This is more D2 than D3.
issues.suggestedFix: Consider re-rating to D2. True D3 comparison questions should involve non-obvious relationships or require additional reasoning steps.

issues.title: No lessons for composite/compound shapes
issues.description: Questions Q126 (L-shape subtraction), Q129 (L-shape addition), Q130 (T-shape), Q131 (L-shape pool), Q139 (step-shape) test composite volume problems but there are no lessons covering the decomposition/subtraction strategy needed. These are mapped to 'master-volume-cuboids' which itself has no lesson.
issues.suggestedFix: Create a new sub-concept 'composite-shapes' with lessons teaching: (1) decomposition into component cuboids, (2) the subtraction method (full shape minus cut-out), and (3) the addition method (adding component volumes). Map Q126, Q129, Q130, Q131, Q139 to this new sub-concept.

issues.title: No lessons for packing/fitting cubes into boxes
issues.description: Questions Q128, Q132, Q133 test packing smaller cubes into larger boxes (dividing each dimension by the cube edge). These are mapped to 'comparing-volumes' but the concept is distinct — it requires understanding that you divide each dimension independently, not divide total volume. No lesson covers this.
issues.suggestedFix: Create a lesson within a new 'packing-cubes' sub-concept teaching the dimension-by-dimension division method. This is a common GL question type.

issues.title: No lessons for displacement and flow rate problems
issues.description: Q121 (flow rate: litres per minute), Q124 (displacement: stone in water), Q125 (fill time), Q134 (water transfer), Q140 (fill time) represent multi-step real-world problem types with no lesson coverage. These are all mapped to 'volume-to-capacity' but require distinct reasoning strategies beyond simple unit conversion.
issues.suggestedFix: Create lessons for: (1) displacement problems (volume displaced = base area x rise in water level), (2) flow rate problems (volume / rate = time). These are high-value GL question types.

issues.title: Missing dimension questions all D2 — no D1 or D3 variety
issues.description: All 13 'missing dimension' questions are rated D2. There should be D1 versions (simple numbers like Volume=24, L=4, W=3, find H) and D3 versions (multi-step or with unit conversion). The current uniform difficulty means the sub-concept never appears in easy or hard quiz modes.
issues.suggestedFix: Add 3-4 D1 missing dimension questions with simple single-digit numbers, and 2-3 D3 versions that combine missing dimension with unit conversion or require finding two missing values.

issues.title: Distractor 150,000 in Q15 has no plausible derivation
issues.description: Q15 options are 60000, 120000, 30000, 150000, 6000. The correct answer is 60000 (half of 120000). 120000 is total volume, 30000 is quarter. 6000 is a magnitude error. But 150000 has no obvious computational path — good distractors should come from plausible mistakes.
issues.suggestedFix: Replace 150,000 with 90,000 (3/4 of total, i.e. picking the wrong fraction) or 24,000 (adding dimensions instead of multiplying: 60+40+50=150 then x some factor).

issues.title: D1 cuboid volume questions are highly repetitive (Q1-Q118 pattern)
issues.description: Approximately 36 D1 questions follow the identical pattern: 'A [box/container/cuboid] is X cm long, Y cm wide, Z cm high. What is its volume?' with only the numbers changing. While drilling is valuable, the lack of variety means a child doing 10 D1 questions will see essentially the same question format every time. No D1 questions test contextual understanding.
issues.suggestedFix: Vary the question format for some D1 questions: use diagrams asking 'Read the dimensions from the picture', present dimensions in a table, or ask 'Which calculation gives the volume?' with formula options. This tests understanding, not just recall of LxWxH.

issues.title: Missing dimension answers cluster around 5 cm
issues.description: Of 13 missing dimension questions (Q4, Q13, Q23, Q37, Q49, Q57, Q67, Q73, Q81, Q90, Q95, Q105, Q110), the answer is 5 cm for 8 of them (62%), and 3 cm for 2, and 6 cm for 3. A pattern-spotting child will learn to always guess 5 cm.
issues.suggestedFix: Vary the missing dimension answers more. Use answers of 2, 3, 4, 7, 8, 9, 10 as well. Currently 5 cm is dramatically over-represented.

issues.title: CuboidDiagram uses 'unit' instead of 'dimUnit' prop
issues.description: In the fraction-of-volume-steps lesson interact screen, the CuboidDiagram bodyParts props use 'unit: "cm"' instead of the standard 'dimUnit: "cm"'. This may cause the unit labels to not render on the diagram.
issues.suggestedFix: Change 'unit' to 'dimUnit' in the CuboidDiagram props at this location.

issues.title: CuboidDiagram in word-problems-mistake interact uses 'unit' instead of 'dimUnit'
issues.description: Same prop name issue as MATHS-VOL-LESSON-001. The interact screen's CuboidDiagram bodyParts use 'unit: "cm"' instead of 'dimUnit: "cm"'.
issues.suggestedFix: Change 'unit' to 'dimUnit' in the CuboidDiagram props.

issues.title: Teach screen has stray period in text
issues.description: The teach screen body text reads: 'Freya only multiplied two dimensions (7 x 4 = 28) instead of all three — that's the area of the base, not the volume!.' — there is an extra period after the exclamation mark.
issues.suggestedFix: Remove the trailing period after the exclamation mark: '...not the volume!'

issues.title: Cube-root lesson sub-concept is orphaned — questions map to volume-cubes instead
issues.description: The cube-root sub-concept has 2 excellent lessons (visual-discovery and key-fact) teaching how to find edge length from volume. However, cube root questions (Q6, Q22, Q31, Q43, Q55, Q64, Q75, Q85, Q96, Q107, Q116) are all mapped to 'volume-cubes' rather than 'cube-root'. This means children doing cube root questions see the basic 'edge x edge x edge' lesson rather than the much more relevant 'cube root' lesson.
issues.suggestedFix: Remap cube-root-type questions (those asking 'A cube has volume X, what is the edge?') from volume-cubes to cube-root. This would also improve the volume-cubes sub-concept by removing reverse questions that don't match its teaching goal.
